_comment: operon-plugin hooks. Phase 6 fix: PreToolUse uses `type: command` (not `type: mcp_tool`) to avoid the chicken-and-egg where every tool call recurses through MCP. The pretooluse-wrapper script resolves a python and invokes pretooluse.py, which reuses operon_mcp_server.rules + guardrail_log.jsonl. Hidden `evaluate` MCP tool stays in the server's registry for in-process testing. Phase 8: Stop hook (also `type: command`) signals the MCP server via a `kind=nudge_check` control envelope dropped into the agent's own mailbox. The MCP watch loop picks up the control envelope and runs nudge.fire_due_nudges() inside the event loop, preserving the SPEC §6.6 single-writer rule on _pending_reply_to.json. PreToolUse matcher Bash|Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit limits firing to side-effectful tools. Stop has no matcher (fires on every turn end).
